title: Agent-recoverable error envelope
description: A transport-neutral error shape an automated client can classify, recover from, and retry safely. Adapt field names to an existing envelope rather than forcing these; the semantics are the contract, not the spelling. Over HTTP this maps cleanly onto RFC 9457 problem details, with these fields as extension members.

code.description: Stable machine identifier. The ONLY field clients branch on. Never renamed or reused with a new meaning once published.

message.description: Human-readable explanation. Mutable prose; clients must never parse or branch on it.

retryable.description: May this condition clear — with time, or after the caller changes something? Says nothing about duplicate safety.

safe_to_retry.description: If the caller repeats the identical request, is a duplicate side effect impossible? For mutations, true requires a nameable idempotency mechanism.

mutation_state.description: What the failed request did to system state. REQUIRED on mutation surfaces. 'unknown' obligates the producer to expose a read-only reconciliation path, named in next_actions.

details.description: Structured, machine-readable specifics (limits, observed values, offending fields). Never credentials, tokens, stack traces, SQL, or internal hostnames. Validation errors SHOULD locate fields with RFC 6901 JSON Pointers.

retry_after_seconds.description: Machine-readable backoff for rate limits and transient conditions.

pointer.description: RFC 6901 JSON Pointer to the offending request field, e.g. "/tags/2".

next_actions.description: Typed, advisory recovery steps. Advice, not authority: consuming agents apply their own policy; producers emit only allowlisted types with structured parameters, never free-text imperatives.

type.description: Allowlisted action identifier, e.g. free_storage, review_tiers, edit_request, reconcile_state.

href.description: Resource or documentation URL relevant to the action.

command.description: A suggested command. Advisory data — a consuming agent validates it against its own policy before use.

why.description: One-line human rationale for the suggestion.

trace_id.description: Correlation identifier joining this public envelope to private logs/traces, so details can stay redacted.
